commit_scope: Make ingest-at-setup the default so the cloud sandbox egress allowlist (the trust list) stops mattering. Adds scripts/form_first_offline_setup.sh — a thin carrier that ingests the repo's own content into the per-clone local lattice, so the Form-first router answers offline without crossing the sandbox boundary. Updates form-first-reasoning.form and CLAUDE.md to make the offline path the default and name why the trust list dissolves when the body travels whole.
evidence_refs: Carrier discipline: scripts/form_first_offline_setup.sh orchestrates existing tools only (coh_substrate.py ingest, the bootstrap substrate CLI) — no new logic; the routing it serves is Form (form-cli-router / form-cli-sufficiency).

Idempotent path proven: seeded one cell (python3 scripts/coh_substrate.py ingest docs/vision-kb/concepts/lc-cognitive-sovereignty.md -> cell_id=406 blueprint=1.7.4.1), then bash scripts/form_first_offline_setup.sh -> printed 'local body already home (lattice populated) — offline Form-first ready, no allowlist needed.' exit 0.

Offline backend proven earlier this session: ingest then query @concept(lc-cognitive-sovereignty)=@1.7.4.1 with no network; ingest --all pulled ~4086 cells incrementally, confirming the lattice persists per-clone (REPO_ROOT = parents[1]).
bash -n scripts/form_first_offline_setup.sh -> OK (syntax valid). chmod +x applied.

Persistence confirmed per-clone: coh_substrate.py roots its store at REPO_ROOT (Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]); separate processes in the same clone read each other's writes; a different clone starts empty (stats 0/0/0).

Platform why: a cloud sandbox's egress allowlist is the platform's (Anthropic/OpenAI), not ours; it only matters while the body is remote. Ingesting the repo's own content at setup means the agent never reaches api.coherencycoin.com — no allowlist, the trust list dissolves. Codex's network model (internet at setup, off during task) makes this the sovereign fit; Claude cloud runs the setup script too.

Edges in the same commit: scripts/INDEX.md + MANIFEST.md regenerated via scripts/generate_repo_indexes.py (--check now clean); form-first-reasoning.form and CLAUDE.md updated to make ingest-at-setup the default and name the trust-list dissolution.

Default scope is concepts (the highest-value 'what is X' body, fast); FORM_FIRST_INGEST=all ingests the whole body. Both degrade gracefully when python deps or toolchain are absent (clear note, falls back to public door / remote).

ci_validation.notes: Docs + a setup-script carrier; no kernel proof bands apply. INDEX --check is clean. CI has not run yet.

deploy_validation.notes: A setup-script carrier and agent-orientation docs; no public HTTP endpoint or service changes. The behavior reaches users when they point a cloud environment's setup script at it (or run it locally).

phase_gate.reason: Local verification passed (idempotent path + INDEX freshness); CI and merge remain pending. No deploy applies.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A cloud/phone agent whose environment runs scripts/form_first_offline_setup.sh at setup gets a populated per-clone lattice and answers Form-first OFFLINE — no egress allowlist (no api.coherencycoin.com reach) required. The trust list is now optional, not necessary.
public_endpoints: none changed; the public substrate read doors remain the networked fallback
test_flows: Idempotent no-op: a populated lattice short-circuits the script to an instant exit with a clear message.
Offline grounding: after ingest the local lattice answers a concept query with no network (proven this session).
